Lines.FullText: If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop.

Elements.RawText: If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop.

Lines.FullText: Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call

Elements.RawText: Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call

Lines.FullText: `IScheduler::runMainLoop()` or `IScheduler::runMainLoopIteration()`.

Description.RawText:
* 
    * If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop. 
    * Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call 
    * `IScheduler::runMainLoop()` or `IScheduler::runMainLoopIteration()`.

Lines.FullText: Checks whether the scheduler will be created with main loop support.

Elements.RawText: Checks whether the scheduler will be created with main loop support.

Brief.RawText: * @brief Checks whether the scheduler will be created with main loop support.

Lines.FullText: True if the scheduler will be configured with main loop support; otherwise, false.

Elements.RawText: True if the scheduler will be configured with main loop support; otherwise, false.

Tags.RawText: @param[out] useMainLoop True if the scheduler will be configured with main loop support; otherwise, false.

Lines.FullText: Enables or disables usage of the scheduler's main loop.

Elements.RawText: Enables or disables usage of the scheduler's main loop.

Brief.RawText: * @brief Enables or disables usage of the scheduler's main loop.

Lines.FullText: Whether to construct the scheduler with main loop support.

Elements.RawText: Whether to construct the scheduler with main loop support.

Tags.RawText: @param useMainLoop Whether to construct the scheduler with main loop support.

Lines.FullText: If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop.

Elements.RawText: If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop.

Lines.FullText: Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call

Elements.RawText: Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call

Lines.FullText: `IScheduler::runMainLoop()` or `IScheduler::runMainLoopIteration()`.

Elements.RawText: `IScheduler::runMainLoop()` or `IScheduler::runMainLoopIteration()`.

Description.RawText:
* 
    * If enabled, the scheduler will be constructed with the main worker, allowing use of the main loop. 
    * Note that enabling this does not automatically start the main loop. To start it, you must call 
    * `IScheduler::runMainLoop()` or `IScheduler::runMainLoopIteration()`.

Lines.FullText: Builder component of Instance objects. Contains setter methods to configure the Instance parameters, such as Context (Logger, Scheduler, ModuleManager) and RootDevice.

Elements.RawText: Builder component of Instance objects. Contains setter methods to configure the Instance parameters, such as Context (Logger, Scheduler, ModuleManager) and RootDevice.

Lines.FullText: Contains a  `build` method that builds the Instance object.

Elements.RawText: Contains a  `build` method that builds the Instance object.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Builder component of Instance objects. Contains setter methods to configure the Instance parameters, such as Context (Logger, Scheduler, ModuleManager) and RootDevice.
 * Contains a  `build` method that builds the Instance object.

Lines.FullText: Configuration Methods:

Elements.RawText: Configuration Methods:

Lines.FullText: The InstanceBuilder provides the following configuration methods:

Elements.RawText: The InstanceBuilder provides the following configuration methods:

Tags.RawText:
@subsection Configuration Methods:
 * The InstanceBuilder provides the following configuration methods:

Lines.FullText: - **Logger:** The custom Logger for the Instance. This logger will be used for logging messages related to the Instance and its components.

Elements.RawText: - **Logger:** The custom Logger for the Instance. This logger will be used for logging messages related to the Instance and its components.

Lines.FullText: If a custom logger is set, the `Logger sink` will be ignored since it is only used with the default Instance logger.

Elements.RawText: If a custom logger is set, the `Logger sink` will be ignored since it is only used with the default Instance logger.

Lines.FullText: If custom logger was not set, builder will generate Instance with default logger.

Elements.RawText: If custom logger was not set, builder will generate Instance with default logger.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Logger:** The custom Logger for the Instance. This logger will be used for logging messages related to the Instance and its components. 
 *   If a custom logger is set, the `Logger sink` will be ignored since it is only used with the default Instance logger.
 *   If custom logger was not set, builder will generate Instance with default logger.

Lines.FullText: - **Global log level:** The Logger global log level for the Instance. All log messages with a severity

Elements.RawText: - **Global log level:** The Logger global log level for the Instance. All log messages with a severity

Lines.FullText: level equal to or higher than the specified level will be processed. Default log level is LogLevel::Default

Elements.RawText: level equal to or higher than the specified level will be processed. Default log level is LogLevel::Default

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Global log level:** The Logger global log level for the Instance. All log messages with a severity
 *   level equal to or higher than the specified level will be processed. Default log level is LogLevel::Default

Lines.FullText: - **Component log level:** The Logger level for a specific component of the Instance. Log messages related to

Elements.RawText: - **Component log level:** The Logger level for a specific component of the Instance. Log messages related to

Lines.FullText: that component will be processed according to the specified log level. By default, each component uses the global log level.

Elements.RawText: that component will be processed according to the specified log level. By default, each component uses the global log level.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Component log level:** The Logger level for a specific component of the Instance. Log messages related to
 *   that component will be processed according to the specified log level. By default, each component uses the global log level.

Lines.FullText: - **Logger sink:** The logger sink to the default Instance logger. This sink will be responsible for processing log messages,

Elements.RawText: - **Logger sink:** The logger sink to the default Instance logger. This sink will be responsible for processing log messages,

Lines.FullText: such as writing them to a file or sending them to a remote server. If `Logger` has been set, configuring of the 'Logger sink' has no effect in building Instance.

Elements.RawText: such as writing them to a file or sending them to a remote server. If `Logger` has been set, configuring of the 'Logger sink' has no effect in building Instance.

Lines.FullText: If logger sinks has not been configure, the Instance uses 'default sinks'.

Elements.RawText: If logger sinks has not been configure, the Instance uses 'default sinks'.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Logger sink:** The logger sink to the default Instance logger. This sink will be responsible for processing log messages, 
 *   such as writing them to a file or sending them to a remote server. If `Logger` has been set, configuring of the 'Logger sink' has no effect in building Instance.
 *   If logger sinks has not been configure, the Instance uses 'default sinks'.

Lines.FullText: - **Module manager:** The custom ModuleManager for the Instance. When configured, the default module manager path will be ignored.

Elements.RawText: - **Module manager:** The custom ModuleManager for the Instance. When configured, the default module manager path will be ignored.

Lines.FullText: If module manager has not configured, the Instance uses built in manager

Elements.RawText: If module manager has not configured, the Instance uses built in manager

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Module manager:** The custom ModuleManager for the Instance. When configured, the default module manager path will be ignored. 
 *   If module manager has not configured, the Instance uses built in manager

Lines.FullText: - **Module path:** The path for the default ModuleManager of the Instance. If a custom module manager has not been set,

Elements.RawText: - **Module path:** The path for the default ModuleManager of the Instance. If a custom module manager has not been set,

Lines.FullText: this path will be used to load modules. Default module path is empty string

Elements.RawText: this path will be used to load modules. Default module path is empty string

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Module path:** The path for the default ModuleManager of the Instance. If a custom module manager has not been set, 
 *   this path will be used to load modules. Default module path is empty string

Lines.FullText: - **Scheduler:** The custom scheduler for the Instance. If set, the number of worker threads will be ignored.

Elements.RawText: - **Scheduler:** The custom scheduler for the Instance. If set, the number of worker threads will be ignored.

Lines.FullText: If scheduler has not been configured, the Instance uses default schduler.

Elements.RawText: If scheduler has not been configured, the Instance uses default schduler.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Scheduler:** The custom scheduler for the Instance. If set, the number of worker threads will be ignored.
 *   If scheduler has not been configured, the Instance uses default schduler.

Lines.FullText: - **Scheduler worker num:** The number of worker threads in the scheduler of the Instance. if a scheduler has not been set and worker num is 0,

Elements.RawText: - **Scheduler worker num:** The number of worker threads in the scheduler of the Instance. if a scheduler has not been set and worker num is 0,

Lines.FullText: which considers as maximum number of concurrent threads.

Elements.RawText: which considers as maximum number of concurrent threads.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Scheduler worker num:** The number of worker threads in the scheduler of the Instance. if a scheduler has not been set and worker num is 0,
 *   which considers as maximum number of concurrent threads.

Lines.FullText: - **Default root device local ID:** The local id of the default client root device. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Elements.RawText: - **Default root device local ID:** The local id of the default client root device. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Default root device local ID:** The local id of the default client root device. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Lines.FullText: - **Default root device info:** The information of default root device of the Instance such as serial number. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Elements.RawText: - **Default root device info:** The information of default root device of the Instance such as serial number. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Default root device info:** The information of default root device of the Instance such as serial number. Has no effect if `Root device` has been congigured.

Lines.FullText: - **Root device:** The connection string of a device that replaces the default openDAQ root device (virtual client).

Elements.RawText: - **Root device:** The connection string of a device that replaces the default openDAQ root device (virtual client).

Lines.FullText: When the instance is created a connection to the device with the given connection string will be established, and the device will be placed at the root of the component tree structure.

Elements.RawText: When the instance is created a connection to the device with the given connection string will be established, and the device will be placed at the root of the component tree structure.

Lines.FullText: When configured, the `Default root device local ID` and `Default root device info` will be ignored.

Elements.RawText: When configured, the `Default root device local ID` and `Default root device info` will be ignored.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Root device:** The connection string of a device that replaces the default openDAQ root device (virtual client). 
 *   When the instance is created a connection to the device with the given connection string will be established, and the device will be placed at the root of the component tree structure.
 *   When configured, the `Default root device local ID` and `Default root device info` will be ignored.

Lines.FullText: - **Sink log level:** The sink logger level of the default Instance logger. This level is ignored if a custom logger has been configured.

Elements.RawText: - **Sink log level:** The sink logger level of the default Instance logger. This level is ignored if a custom logger has been configured.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - **Sink log level:** The sink logger level of the default Instance logger. This level is ignored if a custom logger has been configured.

Lines.FullText: - **Config provider:** The config provider is expanding the local options of instance builder from json file or command lines.

Elements.RawText: - **Config provider:** The config provider is expanding the local options of instance builder from json file or command lines.

Lines.FullText: If value was set before, provider will override this with new one.

Elements.RawText: If value was set before, provider will override this with new one.

Tags.RawText:
* 
 * - **Config provider:** The config provider is expanding the local options of instance builder from json file or command lines.
 *   If value was set before, provider will override this with new one.

Lines.FullText: - **Module options:** Local options dictionary of instance builder has `modules` key which contains custom values for modules.

Elements.RawText: - **Module options:** Local options dictionary of instance builder has `modules` key which contains custom values for modules.

Lines.FullText: By default this dictionary is empty, but can be populated from json file, env variables or command line arguments.

Elements.RawText: By default this dictionary is empty, but can be populated from json file, env variables or command line arguments.

Tags.RawText:
* 
 * - **Module options:** Local options dictionary of instance builder has `modules` key which contains custom values for modules.
 *   By default this dictionary is empty, but can be populated from json file, env variables or command line arguments.

Lines.FullText: The InstanceBuilder provides a fluent interface for setting various configuration options for an Instance

Elements.RawText: The InstanceBuilder provides a fluent interface for setting various configuration options for an Instance

Lines.FullText: object before its creation. It allows customization of the logger, module manager, scheduler and root device.

Elements.RawText: object before its creation. It allows customization of the logger, module manager, scheduler and root device.

Lines.FullText: Once configured, calling the `build` method returns a fully initialized Instance object with the specified settings.

Elements.RawText: Once configured, calling the `build` method returns a fully initialized Instance object with the specified settings.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The InstanceBuilder provides a fluent interface for setting various configuration options for an Instance
 * object before its creation. It allows customization of the logger, module manager, scheduler and root device. 
 * Once configured, calling the `build` method returns a fully initialized Instance object with the specified settings.
